description: The Changling Tomb, the largest tomb complex among the Ming Dynasty imperial mausoleums, is the burial site of Emperor Yongle Zhu Di.
The central square of Beijing, the capital of the People's Republic of China, is the largest city square in the world.
A historic and cultural district in Beijing where the charm of hutongs blends with scenic lakes, offering enchanting night views.
The Olympic swimming venue with a unique membrane structure, now transformed into the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympic Games, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of the Olympic spirit.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, a hub for international brands and the center of modern urban nightlife.
One of Beijing's most famous commercial streets, a century-old street blending traditional and modern commercial culture.
A top-tier science and engineering university in China, known for its beautiful campus and strong academic atmosphere.
A cultural and leisure district in Beijing, featuring creative boutiques, cafés, and unique restaurants.
A quintessential example of ancient Chinese imperial gardens, a UNESCO World Heritage Site centered around Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.
